proc.description: Defines a new Tcl procedure (function) with a name, formal argument list supporting defaults and variable-length args, and a body script that executes when the procedure is called.

set.description: Sets a variable to the specified value and returns it, or returns the current value when called without a value argument, serving as the fundamental variable assignment command.

puts.description: Writes a string to the specified output channel (defaulting to stdout) followed by a newline unless -nonewline is specified, used for all text output in Tcl programs.

gets.description: Reads a line of input from a channel, storing it in varName if provided and returning the character count, or returning the line directly if no variable name is given.

string.description: Ensemble command providing comprehensive string manipulation operations including comparison, searching, matching, transformation, formatting, and character classification subcommands.

length.description: Returns the number of characters in the string.

range.signature: string range string first last -> string
range.description: Returns a substring from the first to last character index inclusive.

match.description: Tests if the string matches a glob-style pattern with * and ? wildcards.

map.description: Replaces substrings according to the key-value mapping list.

trim.description: Removes leading and trailing characters (default whitespace) from the string.

tolower.description: Returns the string converted to lowercase.

toupper.description: Returns the string converted to uppercase.

index.description: Returns the character at the specified index position in the string.

first.description: Returns the index of the first occurrence of the needle in the haystack, or -1.

replace.signature: string replace string first last [newString] -> string
replace.description: Replaces characters from first to last index with the new string.

list.description: Creates a properly-formed Tcl list from the given arguments with correct quoting and escaping, serving as the primary list constructor for building data structures.

array.description: Ensemble command for manipulating associative arrays with subcommands for getting, setting, querying existence, iterating names, and converting between arrays and key-value lists.

set.signature: array set arrayName {key1 val1 key2 val2 ...}
set.description: Sets multiple elements of an associative array from a key-value list.

get.description: Returns the array contents as a flat key-value list, optionally filtered by pattern.

names.description: Returns a list of all key names in the array, optionally filtered by glob pattern.

exists.description: Returns 1 if the named variable is an array, 0 otherwise.

size.description: Returns the number of elements in the associative array.

unset.description: Removes elements matching the pattern from the array, or all if no pattern given.

file.description: Ensemble command for filesystem operations including path manipulation, file attribute queries, existence testing, copying, renaming, and deleting files and directories.

exists.description: Returns 1 if the specified file or directory exists, 0 otherwise.

join.description: Joins path components using the platform-appropriate separator.

dirname.description: Returns the directory portion of the file path.

tail.description: Returns the filename portion after the last directory separator.

extension.description: Returns the file extension including the leading dot.

size.description: Returns the size of the file in bytes.

delete.description: Deletes the specified files or directories, with -force for non-empty directories.

regexp.description: Tests if a string matches a regular expression pattern and optionally captures matched subexpressions into variables, returning 1 on match and 0 on no match.

namespace.description: Manages Tcl namespaces for organizing commands and variables into hierarchical scopes, preventing name collisions and enabling modular package design with import and export control.

eval.description: Evaluates a script in the context of the specified namespace, creating it if needed.

export.description: Specifies which commands in the namespace are available for import by other namespaces.

import.description: Imports commands from other namespaces into the current namespace.

current.description: Returns the fully-qualified name of the current namespace.

package.description: Manages Tcl packages for code distribution and version control, supporting require for loading dependencies and provide for declaring package availability with semantic versioning.

require.description: Loads a package by name, optionally requiring an exact or minimum version.

provide.description: Declares that the current script provides the named package at the given version.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).

null.description: Represents the intentional absence of any value.

undefined.description: Represents an uninitialized or missing value.

print.description: Output a value to the standard output stream.

length.description: Returns the number of elements in a collection or characters in a string.
